questions.question: Consider a simple neural network with one input, one hidden unit, and one output layer. The cost function is defined as the mean squared error between the predicted and actual output values. Implement the Adagrad algorithm from scratch to optimize the weights in this network. Assume the initial value of the weights is 1 and the learning rate is 0.1. Calculate the weights after 10 iterations.?

Given a neural network as in the previous question, with the following weights and biases: w1 = 2, w2 = 0.5, b1 = 1, b2 = 0.2. The cost function is the same as before. Use the Adagrad algorithm to optimize the weights, starting with the initial values w1 = 1, w2 = 0.5. Calculate the new values of w1 and w2 after 5 iterations, assuming a learning rate of 0.1.?
Explain the key advantages of using Adagrad over other gradient descent methods when the number of training examples is very large.?
